violations.description: Mechanical cleaning and sanitizing requirements. — --Sanitize solution for mechanical warewashing machine was not maintained at the proper concentration * . - Sanitizer for the dishwasher was empty.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…)* were not clean or not cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. - Ceiling vents over the food prep area dirty.

Duties of person in charge. — --Person in charge did not ensure employee compliance with an aspect of the food service operation specified in the food service code. Person in charge did not adequately train cook on cooking and cooling procedures. Cook does not check chicken for temperature before placing in the refrigerated prep table. Temperatures should be checked to assure items are cooked adequately. Chicken and other items that are cooked and will be cold held in the prep tables should be cooled in a secondary location before adding to the tables. This aids in proper cooling of the food items and helps the table maintain temperature easier.

General employee cleanliness. — --Food employee did not wash hands as necessary. Observed food employee putting on gloves without first washing his hands. Hands should be washed before putting on gloves.

Preventing contamination by employees. — --Employee's single-use gloves were not discarded after being damaged or soiled. Food employee was observed touching his clothing with gloved hands, and did not change out his gloves before continuing with food preparation. Gloves should be changed after touching personal items and other items not used in food service.

Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Freshly cooked chicken was placed on top of chicken already in the refrigerated prep table that was prepared 5 days ago. Foods prepared on different days should be kept separate or all must be discarded by the original date.

Single-service and single-use articles. — --Single-service item was not stored in a proper location to be protected from contamination. Foam to-go containers and plastic lids were found on the floor of the storage area during inspection. Single use items should be stored off of the floor.
Handwashing, good hygienic practices (5 pts deducted)
Bare hand contact avoided, single- use gloves, and utensils used (3 pts deducted)
Available, adequate capacity, able to maintain proper temperature (4 pts deducted)
Cleaning frequency, maintained clean (2 pts deducted)
Design, construction, clean, maintained (1 pt deducted)
Clean, employee dressing area, cleaning equipment storage, living/sleeping quarters separate (1 pt deducted)
Bare hand contact avoided, single- use gloves, and utensils used (3 pts deducted)
Designed, constructed, installed, and maintained (2 pts deducted)
